question_text: A seesaw has a pivot point at its centre. A child of mass 40 kg sits 1.5 m to the left of the pivot. Another child of mass 35 kg sits 1.8 m to the right of the pivot. A third child of mass 30 kg then sits 0.8 m to the left of the pivot. Calculate the net moment about the pivot and determine which side will go down. Use g = 10 N/kg. Show your working.
correct_answer: Left side: clockwise moment = 600 + 240 = 840 Nm. Right side: anticlockwise moment = 630 Nm. Net moment = 210 Nm clockwise. The left side goes down.
explanation: To find the net moment, calculate the clockwise moments (left side) and anticlockwise moments (right side) separately using moment = force × distance. The child's weight is mass × g, and the side with the larger total moment will go down.
